compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: RESOLVED BY DESIGN — not needed. Joe's adjudication of the gate's T12 finding: the gate is CONFIG-GATED and fail-open when off, so disabling it via config IS the kill-switch/rollback — turning the config off reverts claim to current behavior instantly. No separate rollback mechanism is needed. The global enable/disable flag is inherent to the gate's config surface; recorded in the epic 5fd2 config Success Criterion. This finding was an advisory (not block) over-call the Pass-2 verifier's 'no-existing-mitigation' check should have softened (config-gating IS the mitigation) — good three-pass calibration data: advisory finding -> human adjudication -> dismissed. Closing won't-do/superseded.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Resolved by design — config-gating is the kill-switch/rollback (Joe's adjudication); global disable flag folded into epic 5fd2 config Success Criterion. No separate rollback needed.". Session: 63ba508a.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G3 (child coverage) / parent 5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa + this child.

VERDICT: No parent acceptance/success criterion is left UNCOVERED on account of this pairing. But this child contributes ZERO net coverage AND is INCONSISTENT with the parent's own settled design — recording one finding so the coverage map is honest.

FINDING — child contradicts the parent it was filed to cover; its lane is already covered by siblings (so it is a dropped/superseded over-call, not a coverage contributor)
- criteria: [G3, G4]
- location: child ce75 §Scope / §Why ("a documented rollback / back-out path", "system-level kill switch", "Deploy ordering for the signature scheme") vs parent 5fd2 Success Criterion #4 + §"Design clarification — rollback is config-off".
- evidence:
  1. PARENT EXPLICITLY SAYS THE OPPOSITE. Parent SC#4: "'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, NOT a deploy-time kill-switch, and NO SEPARATE ROLLBACK MECHANISM IS NEEDED." Parent §Design summary likewise: the gate's posture makes config-off the rollback. This child's premise ("a gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure"; demands "a documented rollback / back-out path" + "system-level kill switch") asserts a gap the parent design already closed by construction.
  2. CHILD CLOSED WON'T-DO/SUPERSEDED. ce75's own resolution comment (Joe's adjudication): "config-gating IS the kill-switch/rollback... No separate rollback mechanism is needed... advisory (not block) over-call the Pass-2 verifier's 'no-existing-mitigation' check should have softened." FORCE_CLOSE with no signature. So the child delivers nothing toward any parent criterion.
  3. THE PARENT CRITERIA IN THIS CHILD'S LANE ARE COVERED BY SIBLINGS:
     - Gate ON/OFF config preference + "off => claim unchanged (fail-open)" (ce75 AC#1) is delivered by sibling 092b-3bb7-4890-4cb9 — added AC "ENFORCE the config preference (defaults OFF): off → claim/transition unchanged; on → the fast signature check gates them" (verify-completion PASS; config.py require_plan_review_for_claim default False, tested). The config TOOL/UX is the separate epic 55de (out of scope per parent).
     - Deploy ordering / old+new coexistence for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + signature events (ce75 AC#2/AC#3 lane) is delivered by sibling db7b-c8fd-190f-4506 — added AC "ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence: ... preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones ... fsck WARNs on newer types ... readers-before-writers" (verify-completion PASS).
- scenarios: A reviewer trusting the registry could read ce75's title ("T12 gap the gate found on itself") and conclude the parent has an unmet rollback/kill-switch criterion. It does not: the parent settled this as config-off (SC#4), and the residual mechanics (preference enforcement on the claim path; event rollout-ordering) are covered by 092b + db7b.
- impact: No coverage gap and no orphaned parent criterion; the risk is a CONSISTENCY/registry-hygiene one — a closed-won't-do child whose framing (parent has an unaddressed rollback gap) directly conflicts with parent SC#4. It should not be read as evidence that the parent's rollback/kill-switch criterion is unowned.
- checklist_item: "[ ] Confirm no parent rollback/kill-switch criterion is left uncovered by ce75's closure: SC#4 ('config-off IS the rollback, no separate mechanism needed') + sibling 092b (enforce gate-off preference on claim path) + sibling db7b (REVIEW_RESULT/signature rollout + readers-before-writers coexistence) together cover this lane; ce75 added nothing and was correctly superseded."
- suggested_fix: Leave ce75 closed-superseded (correct disposition). For registry honesty, the parent already records this in SC#4 + the "rollback is config-off" Design clarification; no parent-criterion remediation is required. If anything, link ce75 as superseded_by/relates_to 092b + db7b so the supersession trail is explicit rather than only in the close comment.

G3 NET: parent acceptance/success criteria in this child's concern-area (gate ON/OFF preference; rollout-ordering/coexistence of the sidecar+signature events) are fully covered — by parent SC#4's design stance plus siblings 092b and db7b — so there is NO uncovered parent criterion to flag against the roster. This child is a superseded over-call, consistent only after recognizing config-off as the parent's intended rollback.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
CONTAINER REVIEW — G4 (child consistency) — parent 5fd2-a7c2-0aec-48fa × this child ce75. 7-mode cross-child interaction sweep; high-confidence + benign-reading filter applied.

VERDICT: ONE high-confidence consistency finding (mode: CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS + SCOPE OVERLAP). The remaining 5 modes are clean. The finding is a registry-hygiene/consistency one, NOT a coverage gap (G3 separately confirmed no uncovered parent criterion).

FINDING — child's premise contradicts the parent's settled design, and its scope is fully owned by two siblings (zero net contribution)
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child ce75 §Why + §Scope ("A gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure"; "a documented rollback / back-out path"; "system-level kill switch"; "Deploy ordering for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + signature events") vs parent 5fd2 Success Criterion #4 + §"Design summary"/§"Design decisions revisited (round 5)" + the "rollback is config-off" clarification.
- evidence:
  1. CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS (the dominant mode). Parent SC#4 states the OPPOSITE of this child's premise verbatim: "'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, NOT a deploy-time kill-switch, and no separate rollback mechanism is needed." The parent's round-5 settled-decisions section and the Design summary both reaffirm config-off AS the rollback. ce75 asserts the gate "has no off-switch" and demands a "system-level kill switch" + a separate "rollback / back-out path" — i.e. it presupposes a gap the parent design closed by construction. This is a direct premise contradiction between a child and its parent.
  2. CONFIRMED-SUPERSEDED. ce75's own resolution comment (Joe's adjudication) concedes the contradiction: "config-gating IS the kill-switch/rollback ... No separate rollback mechanism is needed ... advisory (not block) over-call the Pass-2 verifier's 'no-existing-mitigation' check should have softened." FORCE_CLOSE with NO signature. So the child delivers nothing and stands only as a superseded over-call.
  3. SCOPE OVERLAP (no net contribution). Every lane ce75 §Scope names is already owned by a sibling:
     - "disable-the-gate config / gate off => claim behaves exactly as today (fail-open)" (ce75 AC#1) is delivered by sibling 092b-3bb7-4890-4cb9 — added AC "ENFORCE the config preference (defaults OFF): off → claim/transition unchanged; on → the fast signature check gates them" (verify-completion PASS; config.require_plan_review_for_claim default False, interface-tested). The config TOOL/UX is separate epic 55de (parent marks it out of scope).
     - "old+new coexistence during rollout (older clones preserve-and-ignore the new events)" + "Deploy ordering for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + signature events" (ce75 AC#2/AC#3 lane) is delivered by sibling db7b-c8fd-190f-4506 — added AC "ROLLOUT / cross-clone coexistence: preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones, fsck WARNs on newer event types, readers-before-writers" (verify-completion PASS; grounded in _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES + is_unknown_newer_type).
- scenarios: A reviewer scanning the registry sees ce75's title ("T12 gap the gate found on itself") and could infer the parent carries an unaddressed rollback/kill-switch obligation that contradicts SC#4, or that ce75 and 092b/db7b both own the disable-config + rollout-ordering lane (overlap/double-ownership). Neither should stand: SC#4 settles the premise, and 092b + db7b own the residual mechanics.
- impact: Consistency / registry-hygiene only — no live ordering gap and no uncovered parent criterion. The risk is that the child's framing reads as a contradiction of the parent's settled SC#4 and as a scope duplicate of 092b/db7b; left unlinked it muddies which child owns the gate-off preference and the event rollout-ordering.
- checklist_item: "[ ] Reconcile ce75's superseded framing with parent SC#4 (config-off IS the rollback; no separate kill-switch) and de-overlap its scope: gate-off-preference enforcement is owned by 092b, sidecar+signature rollout-ordering/coexistence by db7b — link ce75 superseded_by/relates_to 092b + db7b so the supersession + single-owner trail is explicit rather than only in the close comment."
- suggested_fix: Leave ce75 closed-superseded (correct disposition). Add explicit superseded_by/relates_to links from ce75 → 092b (config-off enforcement) and ce75 → db7b (sidecar+signature rollout-ordering / old-new coexistence) so the registry shows the contradiction was resolved-by-parent-design and the residual lanes are singly owned. No parent-criterion remediation is required (SC#4 + 092b + db7b already cover the lane).

7-MODE SWEEP (the other 5 — clean):
- Implicit shared state: the only shared artifact ce75 touches is the gate-disable config flag, which is the same single flag 092b enforces (no SECOND, conflicting flag is introduced) — no implicit/uncoordinated shared state. CLEAN.
- Dependency gap: ce75 AC#3 ("epic 5fd2 spec references this rollout/rollback design") would imply a producer→parent edge, but the parent settled the matter independently (SC#4), so no missing live dependency edge. CLEAN.
- Ordering violation: ce75 is closed/superseded and ships nothing into the build order; the risk-ordered MVP spine (012e/ca03/1913/acc1/487d/4bb7/092b) is unaffected. CLEAN.
- Consumer impact: ce75 changes no producer surface beyond the config flag already owned by 092b; no sibling consumer is broken. CLEAN.
- Residual references: ce75 AC#3's "spec references this design" is the only dangling reference; it resolves to the closed-superseded state and is addressed by the suggested superseded_by links above (folded into the finding, not a separate mode). CLEAN otherwise.

G4 NET: one high-confidence consistency finding (conflicting-assumptions vs parent SC#4 + scope-overlap with 092b/db7b, zero net contribution, correctly superseded); the other 5 interaction modes are clean.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Reopened to certify its closure (it was previously closed WITHOUT a completion signature, blocking the epic's child-closure check). The work is delivered: (1) DISABLE-CONFIG / kill-switch = the verify.require_plan_review_for_claim config preference (default OFF; turning it off IS the rollback — an ordinary preference, not a deploy-time kill-switch, per the epic's own clarification), implemented in config.py + the claim precheck (092b) and documented in docs/plan-review-gate.md (Configuration / 'turning it off is the rollback'). (2) ROLLOUT-ORDERING for the signature scheme = the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar follows rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones rollout (NOT in KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES; in _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES so fsck recognizes it; older clones preserve-and-ignore until upgrade — upgrade reconcile hosts first), and the plan-review attestation REUSES the existing SIGNATURE event (no new signing event type to roll out). db7b + reducer/_version + event_append cover this.

compiled_state.description:
## What
The three-pass final-gate run on epic 5fd2 (its own authoritative spec, all overlays) surfaced — and a separate verifier confirmed (advisory, confidence .71, severity critical) — a real T12 rollout/rollback gap the five prior review rounds missed: the gate introduces a **blocking change to the in-use `claim` (open->in_progress) path** but never specifies how to **disable/roll back the gate itself**, nor the deploy ordering for the signature/REVIEW_RESULT-sidecar scheme.

## Why
A gate with no off-switch is a single point of failure for every claim across the diverse client base; a botched rollout of the signing scheme could block all claims with no fast revert. The gate's coaching posture + `--force` per-claim is not a system-level kill switch.

## Scope
- A project-level **disable-the-gate** config (gate off => claim behaves exactly as today; fail-open).
- A documented **rollback / back-out** path for enabling the gate (and for the signature scheme), with old+new coexistence during rollout (older clones preserve-and-ignore the new events — cross-check with the TAG_DELTA/event-rollout note).
- Deploy ordering for the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar + signature events vs the claim-path check.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A config flag disables the plan-review gate so `claim` is unchanged (fail-open) when off; covered by a test.
- [ ] A written rollback/back-out plan for enabling the gate and the signature scheme, with deploy-ordering + old/new coexistence documented.
- [ ] The epic 5fd2 spec references this rollout/rollback design (closes the T12 gap the gate flagged on itself).
compiled_state.title: Plan-review gate: kill-switch/disable-config + rollout-ordering for the signature scheme (T12 gap the gate found on itself)
